column1: This dictionary documents the sample retail star schema. Each CSV contains 50 records so the repository can be reviewed without external data dependencies.

column4: Surrogate key for each sales transaction line.
Business order identifier from POS/e-commerce source.
Date of sale linked to DimDate.
Selling store or fulfillment location linked to DimStore.
Region of sale linked to DimRegion.
List price at time of sale.
Promotion or markdown applied to the sales line.
Tender type used by the customer.
Store, web, mobile app, or marketplace.
Surrogate key for each return transaction.
Return transaction date linked to DimDate.
Store or channel accepting the return.
Refund value associated with the return.
Reason captured by service or POS team.
Refunded, pending, store credit, or rejected.
Surrogate key for an inventory snapshot.
Product counted in inventory linked to DimProduct.
Store where stock is held linked to DimStore.
Regional market for the inventory location.
Sellable inventory available at snapshot time.
Buffer stock required to absorb demand variability.
CRM segment used for marketing and retention.
Date the customer joined the loyalty/customer program.
Surrogate key for each store or fulfillment node.
Store name shown to operations leaders.
Smart date key in YYYYMMDD format.
Fiscal year assuming April-March fiscal calendar.
